summary: DHH announces that RubyConf '05 has sold out at 195 attendees, more than tripling the previous year's turnout. He frames the sellout as evidence of Ruby's rapidly growing popularity and momentum. The post closes with a bold prediction that RubyConf '06 will draw 800 attendees, signaling his confidence in Ruby's explosive trajectory.
key_points: RubyConf '05 sold out at 195 attendees, tripling the prior year's numbers
DHH frames the sellout as proof that Ruby is 'burning-red hot' as a technology
He makes a bold prediction of 800 attendees at the next year's conference, quadrupling again
money_quotes: There's certainly no mistaking that Ruby is burning-red hot.
I lavishly predict that we'll see 800 people at RubyConf '06. Watch it happen.
key_insight: The tripling of RubyConf attendance to 195 signaled the beginning of Ruby's breakout moment, and DHH wasn't shy about predicting even more explosive growth ahead.
